title: SGLT2 inhibitors and heart failure outcomes: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
authors: Patel R, Kumar S, Singh A, Desai N
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
abstract: BACKGROUND: SGLT2 inhibitors have shown benefit in heart failure. OBJECTIVES: To assess SGLT2 inhibitor efficacy on mortality and hospitalization in HFrEF and HFpEF. METHODS: Meta-analysis of RCTs (DAPA-HF, EMPEROR-Reduced, EMPEROR-Preserved, DELIVER). RESULTS: SGLT2 inhibitors reduced cardiovascular death or HF hospitalization (HR 0.77, 95% CI 0.71-0.84) across ejection fraction spectrum. CONCLUSIONS: SGLT2 inhibitors benefit patients with HF regardless of ejection fraction.

title: Dapagliflozin in patients with heart failure and reduced ejection fraction (DAPA-HF): primary results.
authors: McMurray JJV, Solomon SD, Inzucchi SE, Kober L, Kosiborod MN
abstract: BACKGROUND: Dapagliflozin, an SGLT2 inhibitor, may reduce risk of worsening heart failure or death. METHODS: Double-blind RCT of dapagliflozin 10mg vs placebo in 4,744 patients with NYHA class II-IV HF and LVEF ≤40%. RESULTS: Primary composite outcome occurred in 16.3% vs 21.2% (HR 0.74, 95% CI 0.65-0.85, p<0.001). Benefits were consistent regardless of diabetes status. CONCLUSIONS: Dapagliflozin reduced risk of worsening HF or cardiovascular death in HFrEF.

title: SGLT2 inhibitors in type 2 diabetes: cardiovascular and renal outcomes.
authors: Zelniker TA, Wiviott SD, Raz I, Sabatine MS
abstract: BACKGROUND: SGLT2 inhibitors reduce major adverse cardiovascular events in type 2 diabetes. We assessed effects across cardiovascular and renal outcomes. METHODS: Meta-analysis of EMPA-REG OUTCOME, CANVAS, and DECLARE-TIMI 58 (n=34,322). RESULTS: SGLT2 inhibitors reduced MACE by 11% (HR 0.89, 0.83-0.96). HF hospitalization reduced by 31% (HR 0.69, 0.61-0.79). INTERPRETATION: Cardiovascular benefit driven by HF reduction, not atherosclerotic events.

title: GLP-1 receptor agonists versus SGLT2 inhibitors for heart failure: a network meta-analysis.
authors: Wong C, Li T, Huang W
abstract: AIMS: To compare GLP-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1 RAs) and SGLT2 inhibitors for heart failure outcomes in type 2 diabetes. METHODS: Network meta-analysis of 15 RCTs. RESULTS: SGLT2 inhibitors were superior for HF hospitalization (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.60-0.77), while GLP-1 RAs showed greater benefit for MACE (HR 0.86, 0.80-0.93). CONCLUSIONS: SGLT2 inhibitors and GLP-1 RAs have complementary cardiovascular profiles.

title: Management of diabetic ketoacidosis with SGLT2 inhibitor use: a clinical practice update.
authors: Goldenberg RM, Berard LD, Cheng AYY
abstract: SGLT2 inhibitors are associated with a small but significant risk of diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA). This practice update provides guidance on recognition, prevention, and management of SGLT2i-associated DKA. We review risk factors, clinical presentation, and treatment algorithms.

title: Empagliflozin and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes (EMPA-REG OUTCOME): a landmark trial.
authors: Zinman B, Wanner C, Lachin JM, Fitchett D, Inzucchi SE
abstract: BACKGROUND: Effects of empagliflozin on cardiovascular outcomes in T2DM. METHODS: RCT of 7,020 patients with T2DM and established CVD randomized to empagliflozin vs placebo. RESULTS: Primary outcome (CV death, MI, stroke) occurred in 10.5% vs 12.1% (HR 0.86, 0.74-0.99, p=0.04). CV death reduced by 38%. HF hospitalization reduced by 35%. CONCLUSIONS: Empagliflozin reduced cardiovascular events and death in T2DM with CVD.
